purpose: Mounts Hugging Face Hub repositories and Buckets as local POSIX filesystems via NFS or FUSE, fetching file content lazily on first read to avoid full downloads.

key_modules: src/virtual_fs/mod.rs — Core VFS engine: inode table, open file handles, POSIX ops dispatch (~2900 lines)
src/virtual_fs/inode.rs — Inode table and metadata management
src/virtual_fs/flush.rs — Batched async flush pipeline for advanced writes
src/virtual_fs/prefetch.rs — Adaptive read-ahead buffer for lazy file reads
src/virtual_fs/poll.rs — Background remote change detection and cache invalidation
src/nfs.rs — NFS filesystem implementation (translates NFS calls to VirtualFs)
src/fuse.rs — FUSE filesystem implementation (translates FUSE calls to VirtualFs)
src/hub_api.rs — Hugging Face Hub HTTP API client
src/cached_xet_client.rs — Cached xet-core CAS (content-addressable storage) client
src/error.rs — Structured Hub error types with retry logic
patterns: Lazy loading — files fetched on-demand via CAS (content-addressable storage), not eagerly downloaded
Adaptive prefetch — sequential reads grow the prefetch window; random seeks use bounded range downloads
Copy-on-write staging — advanced writes download remote content to local staging file, mutate locally, then flush async
Streaming writes — bounded mpsc channel decouples sync FUSE write() from async CAS upload with CommitState state machine
Thundering herd prevention — per-directory tokio Mutex serializes concurrent lazy directory loads
Negative lookup cache — TTL-based cache (30s, 10K capacity) prevents repeated API calls for non-existent paths
Lock ordering discipline — documented hierarchy (dir_loading_locks → inode_table → open_files → negative_cache) prevents deadlocks
Dual backend abstraction — VirtualFs is backend-agnostic; NFS and FUSE modules translate protocol-specific calls to VirtualFs ops
Background polling — periodic remote change detection with conditional HEAD requests and kernel cache invalidation callbacks

concepts_for_deepflow: Lazy content loading: deepflow specs could adopt a similar pattern where full spec context is loaded on-demand rather than eagerly — only sections relevant to the current task phase get resolved, reducing context window pressure

Adaptive prefetch for agent context: like hf-mount's adaptive read-ahead that grows for sequential access, deepflow's execute command could prefetch related files when it detects an agent reading sequentially through a module, but back off for scattered reads

Negative lookup cache for file impact analysis: df:plan's LSP-based impact analysis could cache negative results (files confirmed to not reference a symbol) to avoid redundant findReferences calls across planning cycles

Staging + flush pipeline for worktree writes: the copy-on-write staging pattern maps to deepflow's worktree execution — agents write to isolated staging (worktree), then a flush/verify gate commits to main, similar to FlushManager's debounced async commit

Thundering herd prevention for parallel task execution: when multiple df:execute agents need the same shared resource (e.g., reading the same spec or running the same build), a per-resource mutex pattern would prevent redundant work

Lock ordering discipline documentation: deepflow's concurrent agent execution could benefit from documenting resource access ordering (spec files → PLAN.md → worktree → main branch) to prevent deadlocks in autonomous mode
